_comment: Standardised backtest cost assumptions (BR-05 / WP-86). spread_points is the FULL bid/ask spread in instrument points; the engine charges half per fill against mid-based bar data. slippage_points is extra adverse points per fill on top of the half-spread. commission_per_side is flat account-currency per fill (index CFDs here are spread-only). Values are conservative desk assumptions from advertised broker minimum spreads plus off-peak widening margin - verify against live demo snapshot quotes before treating as final (see docs/backtest-cost-assumptions.md).

US500.cash.notes: Advertised min spread ~0.4; 0.5 covers typical widening outside US cash session.

AU200.cash.notes: Advertised min spread ~1.0; 1.2 covers SYCOM/off-peak widening.

US500.cash.notes: IG US 500 min spread 0.4 in-hours; 0.6 as an average across sessions.

AU200.cash.notes: IG Australia 200 min spread 1.0 in-hours; wider off-peak.

US500.proxy.notes: Proxy data backtests should charge the costs of the venue we would trade on; mirrors forexcom US500.cash.

AU200.proxy.notes: Mirrors forexcom AU200.cash for the same reason.
